title: Fear of Failure as a Driving Force
description: Fear of failure often drives perfectionism, procrastination, or avoidance of new challenges. It can stem from early experiences where mistakes were heavily criticized or where self-worth became tied to achievement.
observation_language: It seems like the fear of failing or not meeting expectations is influencing many of your decisions and behaviors.
There appears to be a pattern of either over-preparing or delaying action due to worry about not doing well enough.
It looks like self-worth may be closely connected to performance and outcomes.
underlying_fears: Fear of not being good enough
